exemplar.title: How has Irelandâs housing crisis exacerbated tensions between its local and migrant communities?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has Irelandâs housing crisis exacerbated tensions between its local and migrant communities?

strengths: Analytical sections are clearly demarcated, guiding the reader
Language is generally clear and the report follows a recognisable structure
weaknesses: Section transitions are abrupt and heading styles are inconsistent
Long paragraphs and run-on sentences occasionally obscure meaning
Introduction lacks a roadmap of the reportâs structure
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is serviceable with a logical overall structure and readable prose, but lapses in clarity, inconsistent formatting and missing navigational cues limit the work to a solid mid-level performance.

strengths: Precise research question clearly frames localâmigrant community tensions
Detailed engagement activities (interviews, site visits) are explicitly linked to the issue
Strong personal motivation and contextual justification enrich the projectâs importance
weaknesses: Occasional digression in subtitle reduces focus on the main question
Justification could more sharply relate project scope to IA word-limit and feasibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student clearly states the issue, provides solid context and personal motivation, and thoroughly links engagement activities to the research question. However, minor clarity lapses (divergent subtitle) and a slightly underdeveloped feasibility justification place this at a strong mid-band level rather than full marks.

strengths: Varied engagement activities planned (NGO interviews, site visits) show forethought
Broad selection of academic and NGO sources demonstrates research planning
Instances where interview insights inform subsequent analysis evidence integration
weaknesses: Logistical and risk-management planning are not systematically documented
No clear timetable or explicit criteria for source/engagement selection
Limited evidence of iterative feedback loops between research and engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission exhibits basic to good planning: appropriate sources and diverse activities are arranged, with occasional research-to-engagement links. However, the absence of detailed logistics, timetabling and feedback mechanisms limits this to the mid-band for Process.

strengths: Balanced, layered analysis of economic, social and policy factors driving the housing crisis
Relevant application of course concepts (e.g. Galtungâs peace triangle, social contract) to interpret tensions
Integration of a human-rights perspective from NGO viewpoints adds theoretical depth
weaknesses: Stakeholder perspectives are presented but not fully woven into a coherent, iterative synthesis
Minor conceptual inaccuracies (e.g. misnaming Galtung) and occasional superficiality in operationalisation
Argument would benefit from quantitative data to substantiate policy claims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis moves beyond description into a clear, insightful examination of multiple causal factors and applies key course concepts with reasonable accuracy. Synthesis of stakeholder views is competent but not fully effective, placing the work in the lower end of the 5-6 band.

strengths: Reflective discussion on developmental-state models and democratic process limitations shows some depth
weaknesses: Evaluation of research and engagement remains broad and unsystematic
Personal biases are acknowledged but not critically examined
Reflections offer limited insight into future implications or methodological improvements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation and reflection are present but remain at a superficial level. The student notes strengths and constraints but does not systematically assess methodologies or deeply explore personal bias and future learning, warranting a mid-band score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student clearly states an explicitly focused questionââHow has Irelandâs housing crisis exacerbated tensions between its local and migrant communities?ââand provides sufficient contextual background on immigration flows, homelessness rates and recent riots.  Explanations are largely precise, although occasional digressions (e.g., a second brief title on human-rights conflict) reduce absolute clarity.  Overall the issue is unambiguously identified and set within a relevant socio-political frame, meriting the upper band but not full marks.

Evidence of planning appears implicitly (early e-mail outreach, selection of academic and NGO sources, compiled bibliography) and most sources are appropriate to the issue.  However, no systematic timetable, research matrix or criteria for source selection is outlined, so the work exceeds minimal but falls short of a âwell-structuredâ plan.

The student arranged multiple interviews and a shelter visit, showing forethought in selecting varied actors.  Yet timing, logistics and risk-management are not articulated; consequently the plan is present but patchy, fitting the middle band.

Course conceptsâGaltungâs violence triangle, positive/negative peace, neoliberalism vs developmental state, Lockeâs social contract, âright to the cityââare applied in relevant sections to interpret causes and policy debates.  Minor inaccuracies (misspelling Galtung; limited operationalisation) prevent the work from being âpurposeful throughoutâ, but usage is generally accurate and meaningful, warranting the 3-4 band at its upper level.

Links between research and engagement are demonstrated when interview insights (e.g., Golden on neoliberalism, Leonard on demographic benefits) re-appear in the analytical section.  Nevertheless, instances are intermittent and there is little evidence of iterative feedback shaping subsequent inquiry, indicating âsometimes informsâ rather than consistent integration.

Engagementsâinterviews with the Simon Community CEO, the Irish Council of Social Housing, an EU migration specialist, and site visits to sheltersâare described in detail, each explicitly linked to aspects of the question (NGO limitations, public perceptions, policy context, empathy with homeless migrants).  The narrative makes clear how every activity generated insights later used in the analysis, demonstrating strong relevance and purpose; thus the strand earns the top of the band.

Language is generally readable, yet clarity dips through run-on sentences, repeated phrases, and minor terminology errors.  These lapses obscure meaning at points, so communication is satisfactory rather than consistently clear.

The analysis moves beyond description to examine multiple, interacting causal layersâeconomic policy, social media polarisation, post-colonial identity, NGO capacityâwhile weighing competing neoliberal vs developmental solutions.  The discussion is balanced and substantiated, though not exhaustively data-driven, aligning with the lower end of the 5-6 âthorough, insightfulâ band.

Personal position is acknowledged (child of immigrants, emotional reaction at shelter), but exploration of how this bias might shape research choices or interpretation is only briefly mentioned and lacks depth, fitting the 1-2 band.

The report follows a recognisable structure (introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography), but section transitions are occasionally abrupt and some material (e.g., split titles, long paragraphs) hampers flow, indicating basic but imperfect organisation.

Evaluation is limited to broad statements (government action âminimalâ, NGOs âstrainedâ).  The student does not systematically assess source reliability, methodological limits or engagement effectiveness, resulting in a largely superficial judgement of activities.

Importance is justified on several concrete grounds: (1) the growing scale of Irelandâs housing shortage, (2) links to rising xenophobia and recent violence, and (3) the studentâs personal stake as a prospective immigrant.  Suitability is discussed through explicit mention of how the topic connects to human-rights and peace-studies themes in the syllabus.  While persuasive, the justification could be sharper in relating the topic to the IAâs word-limit and feasible depth, so a solid 3 rather than 4 is appropriate.

Stakeholder views (immigrants, native citizens, NGOs, government, firms) are presented and occasionally compared (e.g., contrasting NGO reliance with public mistrust).  Some connections are explained, yet the perspectives are not fully woven into a coherent, iterative argument, placing the work at a competent but not âeffective synthesisâ level.

Reflection notes empathy gained and appreciation of complexity, yet insights remain surface-level and do not articulate implications for future inquiry or action.  Hence the reflection is âlimitedâ, meriting 2.

annotations.comment: The site visit outline lacks mention of selection criteria. Include rationale to demonstrate a structured engagement plan.
Conclusion effectively reiterates the interplay of housing as a human rights issue and conflict potential.
The Corporate Europe Observatory entry lacks full title details; verify completeness of sources for reliability assessment.
Long URLs break formatting and readability; use hyperlinking or footnotes to streamline references.
An extensive range of academic and NGO sources demonstrates commendable research breadth and planning.
The research question is precise and clearly frames the issue within local vs. migrant community tensions.
The subtitle âPolitical Issue: How do human rights cause conflict?â diverges from your main question. Refine or remove to avoid confusion.
Integrate this image reference into the text analysis to strengthen the connection between visual evidence and your argument.
Add brief commentary on the protest imagery to demonstrate how public sentiment is visualized in your study.
The introduction heading is clear but lacks a roadmap of sections, which would improve navigation through the report.
This paragraph conflates multiple pointsâeconomic migration, asylum, homelessnessâimpeding clarity. Break it into distinct thematic sentences.
Consider adding specific data or citations on the Dublin Riots to substantiate and enrich this contextual discussion.
Personal motivation adds depth and relevance, effectively justifying project importance through lived experience.
The header formatting is inconsistent with other sections. Standardize heading styles for improved coherence.
This engagement description details logistics but omits reflection on how insights shaped subsequent research. Add notes on feedback loops.
Articulation of the human rights dimension from the NGO perspective adds theoretical richness.
Clear demarcation of the analytical section enhances structure and guides the reader effectively.
When referencing Leonardâs points, link explicitly to course concepts (e.g., demographic dividend) to deepen conceptual application.
The explanation of Galtungâs peace triangle contains a misnomer (âGautengâ). Correct to âGaltungâ to maintain theoretical accuracy.
This section is cut off; ensure completeness of analysis to avoid losing critical argument threads.
Synthesize the post-colonial trauma perspective more tightly with human rights theory to avoid it feeling like an isolated point.
Effective integration of Lockeâs social contract shows adept use of political theory to explain modern protest dynamics.
The definition of neoliberalism is accurate but could benefit from quantitative examples (e.g., housing price inflation rates) to ground the argument.
Expand on the contrast between neoliberal and developmental state models by incorporating a brief case study or historical precedent.
Discussion on developmental state models bridges theory and policy, demonstrating reflective depth on political structures.
Claims about minimal government action lack supporting data; include examples of policy measures or budget figures to substantiate.
Critically reflect on NGO constraints by assessing their resource limitations and proposing improvements to stakeholder coordination.
Insightful evaluation of democratic process limitations demonstrates reflective depth on political structures and their constraints.
The conclusion header is understated; add a transitional sentence referencing the initial question for a stronger closure.
Ensure consistent citation style; the bibliography currently mixes formats and omits access dates.
